evals.prompt: New fullstack project: React frontend, Node API, PostgreSQL. 3 users: me (lead), Alice (frontend spec), Bob (API spec). We disagree on what 'signup complete' means. Use consensus mode to generate plan.
evals.expected_output: Runs 3 parallel analyses: Alice focuses on form UX (email validation, error messages, redirect to dashboard); Bob focuses on API (create user record, return JWT, check DB insert); I focus on integration (form → API → DB → frontend shows user name). Merges into single 'signup journey' with 7 steps covering all 3 perspectives. Writes to e2e-evidence/validation-plan.md. Mode: consensus.

evals.prompt: Express API with 12 endpoints. I can't validate all of them in one cycle. How do I prioritize journeys in the plan?
evals.expected_output: Directs to risk classification: auth endpoints (login, refresh token) = HIGH → must validate; payment endpoints = HIGH → must validate; CRUD for secondary resources = MEDIUM → validate sample. Generates quick mode plan: 4-5 critical journeys covering auth + payments. Identifies gaps: 'CRUD for Orders' marked MEDIUM, deferred to next cycle. Justifies priorities in plan header.
